THE BEAVER COUNTY EMERGENCY MANAGERS OFFICE REPORTED THAT THERE WAS DOWNBURST WIND DAMAGE IN TURPIN. THERE WAS DAMAGE TO GARAGES THAT WERE BUILT ON TO TRAILER HOMES AND A CHURCH HAD A TREE UPROOTED SEVERAL TREE LIMBS HAD BROKEN OFF AND THERE WERE POWER POLES AND POWER LINES DOWN. HE SAID THAT THIS DAMAGE OCCURRED APPROXIMATELY 7 PM CDT.

2 OUTBUILDINGS WERE DESTROYED...IRRIGATION EQUIPMENT OVERTURNED...AND TREES WERE DOWNED. ESTIMATED 70 MPH WINDS.

A TOTAL OF FIVE HOG FARMS WERE DAMAGED BY STRONG WINDS. TWO OF THE HOG FARMS WERE SIGNIFICANTLY DAMAGED WITH THE ROOFS BLOWN OFF WITH DAMAGE TO THE SIDE OF THE BUILDINGS. ANOTHER HOG FARM IS MISSING PART OF THE ROOF WHILE THE OTHER TWO HOG FARMS HAD LESSER DAMAGE TO THEIR ROOFS. ALL FIVE HOG FARMS ARE STILL STANDING BUT THERE IS A LOT OF DEBRIS LAYING AROUND. NUMEROUS TREES WERE BLOWN OVER. THE DEBRIS AND TREE DAMAGE WAS LOCATED ON HIGHWAY 23 ON THE WEST AND EAST SIDES OF THE HIGHWAY.
